Whilst I wouldn't call Marvel's Spider-man 2 on-par with the Quality of the 1st or 2nd (Miles Morales-based) games, it's no slugfest by any means.
Featuring spectacular graphics, superb voice acting, and really enjoyable mechanics and customisability, there are aspects of this game that absolutely improve on previous flaws of it's predecessors; but, unfortunately, the story and open world lack some of the meat and potatoes that made the first two games so thoroughly enjoyable, weakening the experience.
Despite this, the game still remains an overall fantastic addition to the Marvel's Spider-man videogame world, and is absolutely worth picking up if you enjoyed what came before. It simply could have done with a bit more time in the oven.

I've played the previous two games on PS4 Pro and PS5 before and I've always been loving the Spider Man Games. I love exploring NYC while doing missions and I can really enjoy the Open World and Atmosphere in these games. Initially the game had a lot of bugs, most are fixed now and I've only experienced 4 crashes while playing. Story is pretty much ok, nothing super special but definitely isn't bad either. World Design is really good, just like in the previous games and the city is usually full of life with many cars and pedestrians. I think it's not as good as the original Spider Man Game on PS4 but is absolutely worth playing. Only thing that annoyed me a bit was that the enemies towards the end of the game became very unbalanced. Some were super hard while others are very easy to fight against, but I really love the combat system in the Spider Man Games! Can recommend for anyone who's played the other two games already!

I saw a lot of people saying that this game, although good, is still worse than SM1, but after clearing the thing I can confidently say that this one is better in essentially all levels.
Map/open world expanded by a lot, I'd say it probably doubled in size since they added Queens and Brooklyn, it's definitely more colourful, vibrant and more detailed.
Story was quite nice with good pacing, didn't let me lose interest, ending on a cliffhanger, setting us up for the third game of the series.
Side content was plenty, enjoyable to complete and didn't feel repetitive whatsoever.
Finally, combat had a major improvement, since Pete has skills now that he can use and there are quite a few, they also added new skill onto Miles' arsenal, for those that did play SM Miles Morales, and last but not least they finally implement gadget shortcuts into the game making them so much easier to use compared to the pain in the bum that it was before.

Feels like a moderate downgrade from the first game, but the core gameplay loop and main character writing is still here. We've just lost a lot outside of that. Went from a 9.5/10 on the first game to an 8/10 here.

There feels like a major disconnect between the characters the story focuses on and the city as a whole. In the first game there was Yuri to act as a pov from the police end, and you generally interacted with civilians quite often. More grounded, down to earth style missions that made you remember why you liked the early MCU have all but vanished here.

In addition, some of the side quests are aggressively bad. I would rather chase thousands of birds than sit through that couples quest at the school again. (Portrayals of non-traditional relationships are more harmful than helpful when written so poorly and I felt second hand embarrassment throughout.) There are also a fair few amount of 'sit and listen' style side missions which... feels like they wanted to showcase Spiderman(s) focus on helping the common folk without wanting to put real effort into it.

Gameplay is generally upgraded across the board; boss fights allow more interactions with your full kit instead of being mostly restricted to damage + webs and characters gain entirely new abilities over the course of the game that allow you to further diversify your playstyle.

There really should be a neutral review option. 8 patches later and the port is seemingly better than it was at launch, only problems I experienced were that the game sometimes launched with no audio so I had to restart it, there were a few visual hiccups here and there but Imma chalk it up to the fact that the system reqs for the game just barely exceed my pc specs (did the whole Scream boss fight in 10 fps :D). But all in all, there weren't any game breaking bugs in my run. As far as story is concerned, it was alright, it lacked the heart that the first game's story had but it's still a good Spider-Man story imo, they adapted the black suit story pretty well and I really liked Kraven as a villain (worth noting that the story avoided the "too many main villains" that Spidey stories outside the comics tend to have despite having both Kraven and Venom).
The gameplay itself is peak Spider-Man. The combat feels better than ever and the web wings are just amazing.

I recommend this game because it is generally a good game despite all of the port's remaining issues, but if you still want to get your money's worth (be it full price or discount) wait some more until the game is fully patched and all.

As someone that grew up with the character- all through the TV shows, movies, comics and even most of the Activision games, this is one of my favourite Spider-man games.

At least in terms of gameplay.

The story needed a couple of rewrites, especially in the Venom part of the game (thanks SONY!!!) and what we got is a similar situation to Mass Effect Andromeda; gameplay was in the oven and cooked to near perfection while the writing was left to defrost and someone forgot about it until the last minute. Overall, started strong, fumbled near the finish line but somehow stuck the landing.

As for the gameplay, I do enjoy the powers for both Peter and Miles. The one caveat for me is the gadgets and I barely used them. Insomniac cooked with what gadgets they gave Peter and Miles in their own games and I wish that most of them had carried over so you could make both Spider-men feel different.

As for this PC port? Besides having this crash on me from being too fast, I run this on an NVMe SSD with a 4060 and AMD Ryzen 5600 so I didn't run into any visual bugs.

Overall, this is a GOOD game but it doesn't have the emotional weight that MSMR and MM had thanks to Sony forcing Insomniac to redo the story at the eleventh hour.

Spider-Man 2 is...ok. And that's kind of the problem with it. As a creator/reviewer who has almost exclusively looked at super hero games, and specifically Spider-Man this game while fine enough, is a disappointment on almost every metric minus the traversal. Combat is a step down relying on cooldowns and worthless gadgets compared to 2018's creative sandbox with it's gadgets and mod systems, in game physics no longer work like how they did previously choosing to instead prioritize clunky mechanics that don't add a lot in the grand scheme of things. I have an entire 50 minute review where I go into great detail about my problems with the story and some gameplay elements. It's fine enough but the game feels like a game made for profit as opposed to being a game made out of love for the character like how SM2018 felt.

Mild spoilers:
This game is amazing. On PC there were some bugs that should've been fixed but whatever. The story is good, not as good was the first game but still good. The gameplay is definitely better, especially with the symbiote suit. The graphics is good too, and the MJ missions aren't bad like the first game because you actually have a weapon. My complaints other than the bugs are that they didn't handle Peter well without the symbiote suit, many times it was just Miles saving Peter, and that the Venom part was very rushed. Overall this game is amazing and I highly recommend playing it.

Yawn.
This is probably one of the most over inflated and tedious games i have dragged myself through. I really enjoyed the first Spiderman game and then the Miles Morales followup was awesome. This game starts out ok but about a third of the way in the game starts to feel like a chore. Or a chore that is so far up it's own a$$ that it just feels frustrating to play. The writing is absolutely terrible. I would rather hit myself in the face with a frying pan multiple times than sit through any of the dialogue in this game again.

There are way too many walk around and do nothing sections that the genius writers clearly thought were amazing bits of character development. One mission has you literally throwing garbage away in your house. While some of the early quests like when you revisit your school with Harry are decent and somewhat essential to the character development the gmae constnatly forces you to endure far too many of these and they are neither fun, well written or remotely interesting to play.

Next you have the horrendously bad MJ missions. Lets not even get into how bad the MJ facial model is this time around. Peter and Miles have fantastic models with some really decent facial animation. They look natural. MJ looks like something broke and her facial performance looks broken at times. The MJ sections are so poorly made and unpolished. It feels like they were outsourced to a cheap third party to develop and then dropped them into the game. I get that they wanted to build her up as part of the story but they really should have put some effort into making them actually play as well as the main characters instead of doing her a disservice like this.

The overall story is decent enough. It has some really nice moments and there are some great set pieces but overall there is far too much bloat and filler and it takes far too long to get to where its going. Kraven is appallingly bad. HIs character makes zero sense. His fight is just stupid and boring. They set him up to be this terrifying badass and he comes across as corny and pathetic for the most part and for some reason despite being this amazing hunter has his crew do all the hunting. Its just total cliche paint by numbers writing.

The third act then has you facing repeated boss fights that all feel exactly the same. One after another. It doesn't feel climactic it feels boring. Oh look another boss fight that is identical to the five you already just did but we gave you a new skill now. How exciting. The game keeps throwing you into situations over and over when all you want to happen is for the game to just get to its conclusion. The actual combat its self becomes tedious. You might as well just be playing a clicker game because it ends up feeling that way. Click click click click dodge, hit a power attack as soon as its charged. Repeat. It just feels really boring by the time the credits roll.

Oh and to make matters worse if you try and focus on the main story so you can get things over with and uninstall to free up 100GB the game won't let you. It keeps stopping you in your tracks and forcing you to go do stupid open world missions until it decides you are worthy of another main story mission.

The overall presentation is great as with the other games. The game is pretty well polished. Performance is fine so long as you don't bother with Raytracing. Main characters control really well and overall it feels the same to play as the other games just with more powers and bigger set pieces. Flying around the city and the various traversal methods the game offers you are also fantastic as with the previous titles.

If you are a huge huge spiderman fan then this could be worth your time, and while this does offer far more than the previous titles it also feels far too similar and far too drawn out to be something that i'd ever want to drag myself through again.

When a game makes you angry playing it because it just won't finish - it's difficult to recommend that game.

After playing the first two games, I can recommend this one as well. The story does not feel as well fleshed out as the first, but the gameplay is fun and the new features added to it are enjoyable.

Playing with an RTX 3070 and Ryzen 5 5600X at 1440p was best experienced at mostly high settings. It is unplayable with ray tracing which involved lots of dips. Some areas of the game lag while others do not. Scenes involving the symbiote and cuts to different camera angles in cutscenes lagged the most for me; so there is still noticeable room for performance improvement. The visuals look great despite there being some visual bugs such as some windows having a Z-fighting appearance. I have only experienced 1 crash which was at the start of my playthrough,

It took me about 28 hours to beat the game and complete all side quests. As great as it is, I personally do not think it is worth paying the full $60 since there is not too much outside the main story, and good consistent performance is still a hit or miss for this port.

I would like to say that at the time of writing this review I have received all but one achievement in the game, Which I only need to beat the main quest in new game+ to get. I am half way or more towards getting that one too. With all that being said, I do have some thoughts.

The game is fun and I think the story, though not as good as the first game, is still really good. The game ran fine, but I did need to make some upgrades to my pc and change some setting to play. One weird thing was I need to underclock my GPU. Really weird but it worked fine afterwards.

The game's gameplay had many improvements compared to the first game. There are less gadgets, but I found myself using every gadget during combat. The enemies will actually notice when people have gone missing when you are taking them down with stealth. When alerted they will even look up and try to spot you, making stealth a lot more interesting and adding an extra layer of challenge (though I still found it fairly easy). The web-wings are very fun to use for traversal. Both Spider-Men have skills that can be used and swapped out during combat and neither Spider-Man feel more powerful than the other.

I will say that I felt that this game lacked content when compared to the first game, but I'm also including the DLC content as well. This one felt much shorter, and you really feel it when you play for a second time and decide to skip the cinematics like I have. Without spoiling, they do cameo or introduce other Spider-Man villains without much purpose. In the first game, you do some tasks for Taskmaster and he will occasionally fight you. In this one, they show at least 3 villains and one of them you sorta "fight" another has missions for but you never directly interact with, and another has an appearance for about 10 seconds and is never seen again. There are a few others that make a short appearance for a mission or two but in the first game it felt like most every mission had a purpose. This one feels like they had a lot of ideas and didn't know how to fit all of them in a smooth way. But overall I think it is still good.

I would recommend this game to anyone with a computer capable to play it. I would say, however, that if you are short on cash, then wait for this to go on sale. I don't think the original price in unreasonable, but there are games with far more content for the same price or cheaper at this sort of quality. I think if you enjoyed the first two games, then you will enjoy this one.

Waited for the bugs to be fixed and tbh, I have only had to restart twice, I believe. I know that shouldn't be the case to begin with, however, it's what we got. Not gonna lie, my rig was expensive but by no means top of the line. If you're looking for a game that just delivers.... this is it.
I love difficult games (not abusive games like souls... okay sometimes I like a little abuse, not the time though) and the scalability on the difficulty in this game is sssoooo nice: want to feel like you are spider-man and have to react like them? Change your transverse webbing in the settings, it feels like an entirely different game. The game does force you to 'get gud' = have well adapted timing or/and use the gadgets you got on hand, which are definitely essential on your first play through (That New game + these hands were rated E for everyone). Once you get into New Game +, the difficulty does slightly disappear and you feel like a powerhouse against the crowds (Playing on Ultimate, no assistance on). Unlocking the skills from your previous play through feels like you are the amazing spider-man. That being said, the game is for everyone(what I mean): you like to throw hands = remember the combos and how to really utilize them against walls, or in the air; like stealth confrontations: go invisibil(Danny phantom reference) and just making people disappear like your Houdini assistant; Are you physicist who want to see what a manhole does to a brutes cranium, they got you covered; the gadgets they give you (insert high moan), reminds me of why I became a scientist to begin with... no seriously. (Nit picky moment: I do wish they would've spoken to a chemist or biochemist about chemical structure in their mini-game.) So what I'm saying is, if you have a particular play style, you can play it, the literal limitation is you... I don't need to tell the gaming world how rare that is.
From just a story perspective, it's amazing (8/10), and honestly I'm being hard on them with that score-- I think they could've given us a little more but it's only because I'm a comic book nerd and know there is so much they could tell. Baring that, the game has so many different mechanics that allows you to feel like you're not just playing a game but immersed, you don't feel like side missions are a chore -- this is an aspect as a gamer, that is really important to me: can you keep my attention without throwing endless side missions and side quests at me that I just decided to ignore them like that one ex ... (which I hate because perfectionism exists in my soul).
Cosmetically, they smacked harder than a dominatrix with something to prove(outside of parental issues): none of that microtransaction bs, pure grind but it doesn't feel like you're grinding because they award you for being a hero and saving NY.
The ability range in the game is maddening: from OG spiderman; the up and coming spiderman; or venom-- yes venom (Sheds a thug tear).

TL/DR: game delivered better than the mythical milk run my dad promised he'd be back from

I really wanted to enjoy this game, but it's borderline unplayable in its current state.

The game crashes in nearly every major story cutscene, and sometimes even during regular combat. I've tried lowering settings, clearing shader cache, disabling overlays, and everything else that’s usually recommended. My hardware meets the recommended specs, and other demanding titles run fine – this one clearly doesn't.

It's honestly frustrating that a AAA developer would release a full-price game in this condition. The lack of meaningful updates since launch makes it even worse.

Until these stability issues are fixed, I can’t recommend this game. It looks great, sure – but what’s the point if it constantly crashes?

For context I have 100% completed the original Spiderman game and Miles Morales.

After 100% completing this game I disagree with many of the reviews saying this game regularly crashes and is unoptimised. In my whole time playing, I had one crash where I had to reload the game, but with the checkpoints I had to replay around 30 seconds of the game so no biggie.

There are a fair few non game breaking bugs but this was the case when I played the prior games and it doesn't detract from the game in any meaningful way. We're talking some NPC pathing issues or strange ragdoll physics but it never broke the game, just looked a little goofy at times.

All in all a good second installment of Spiderman even if I felt the story was slightly weaker than the first, there is enough content here to feel like I've got my money's worth.

Side note: I do recommend playing the two games set before this as it does follow on from those and there will be many characters that you have zero context for without playing the prior games.

The story and gameplay are awesome, and there's a lot of cool extra collectibles and activities around the map, and, as usual, awesome suits, with the bonus of most of them having multiple color combinations (so you can make Pete's default suit yellow, for example). Once you get into the New Game + stuff, things get a little rocky. For example, any time Peter and Miles get a new suit, whatever cutscene that plays doesn't actually reflect the new suit (for example, Miles shows up to meet Pete and Pete comments how cool Miles' new suit is, but Miles is just wearing the same suit he had before the cutscene). There's some new combat mechanics that get introduced for Peter partway through the game (trying not to spoil anything here) but in NG+, he'll start with those, and if you turn them off, he won't get them when he's actually supposed to, and you'll have to switch them back on in the menu. There's also some weirdly timed side missions (not just in NG+), so you'll get a call from someone asking for help, and you can either ignore them and come back later, or go there right away. If you go right to the side mission, it'll be mid day, but when you got the call 5 minutes ago, it was the middle of the night. I'm not superannoyed about this, and I'd expect this if I skipped the mission and came back, but it's a little shocking to the senses to just have the time of day change like that for one mission, then just change back to how it was before, especially if I played the mission right when I got it, which I normally do in games. Also, NG+ tries to bring in some other little extras, like the option to equip golden versions of your web gadgets, many of which you never even see anyway, and being able to equip both abilities in the skill tree that you could previously only choose one (so you get the option of either ability A or B, but now you can use both at once, if you buy them again), and new color variations on suits specific to the story that only had one previously, but it takes more than one 100%ed NG+ playthrough to actually unlock that all, so that's a little weird. There's a lot of other half-baked things that could be pretty awesome for the second main game in the franchise, and I love the ideas they had, but a lot of them just didn't end up fully thought through and properly executed -- of course none of these issues make the game unplayable, they just break the immersion and give me the ick for a few minutes. Overall, definitely get this game if you liked the first two, but don't be surprised when there are very minor hiccups in later playthroughs.

OK its fun I admit......sometimes. Spider-Man is a beloved character of mine dating as far back as Spider-Man on the PlayStation 1, however, this new entry in the franchise missed so many marks from the short story, to the even shorter 3rd act, lack of character emotional depth, or even major lack of high stake situations when *SPOILER* everyone lives apart from the villains essentially stripping any major impacts within this story for our Hero. The black suit, its "fine" to put it lightly, really you play the same 3 animations over and over, to make it worse his standard light and heavy attacks are the same as his Red, White, and Blues (I mean look at Web of Shadows, Black Suit Spidey feels like a completely separate character with the use of tenderals and such). The biggest let down of most was Venom, he feels like this massive threat you are anticipating and anxious to fight for a majority of the game, but then hes only in the last act which remember is short as ****, hardly even a villain in the game CENTERING AROUND THE SYMBIOTE HIMSELF (Although his final boss fight is rather welcoming). I mean decades old game like Web of Shadows and Ultimate do a far better job with Venom and his plot and what sucks the most is we won't see a venom/black suit Spider-Man for a while again, so this is what we get for the next few years or more. It has a few moments that keep you on the edge but it goes away quickly when you realize Spidey doesn't have to face any real consequences.

In the end, its a bit heartbreaking because Spider-Man 2018 set some high bars that I was sure Insomniac would surpass utilizing Spideys biggest villain, but that's hardly the case. I would recommend buying it on sale for around $30, reasoning is the game feels half-baked, the story is a bit short for what you'd expect, and a lack of side content that's included.

Poor main story that rushes through the more interesting moments and rehashes old ones but good minor gameplay and traversal improvements and a further improved recreation of New York to swing around in that make it enjoyable to play through.

The main storyline of the game involves Kraven and his group of hunters going after the super heroes and villains of New York while also dealing with the reappearance of Harry Osborn and the black symbiote suit that is both needed to counter Harry's illness and effects Peter's mind when merging with him. It is a massive downgrade compared to the first game as it often revolves around every character being whiny and incompetent while refusing to have any real stakes added to many of the situations or leads to character's making up what should be non existent problems.

One of the main issues Peter, Miles, and MJ face in the plot is their future careers with Peter unable to hold a job, Miles focusing on being Spider-Man over getting into college (which seems to be ignored with the game's ending), and MJ struggling to be a reporter now that her new boss is Jonah Jameson. The thing is, all of those problems are asinine. The series really leans into Peter being an amazing scientist, he does not need a job, he can clearly easily have a side career patenting some thing or doing some 3D prints of some things he makes or he can go ask the Avengers or Fantastic Four to use their equipment for something, a job is not a real problem that he should have to worry about and it is why most things moved on to his life being terrible in other ways rather than just financially. At one point he uses his web to bandage and apply pressure to a stab wound, create a form of that for medical use and sell it to someone. Miles pointless college dilemma brings me back to some 90s sitcom Fresh Prince/Boy Meets World propaganda of, "You go to college boy or your future is worthless, you are worthless!" Miles does not need to go to college for some music tech audio mixing career, and the real problem is that he is just putting it off like a normal person does, it clearly has nothing to do with him being Spiderman for why he can't write 500 words. Whatever Ganke can do with technology is clearly far beyond anything where he should have to worry about a career or college. MJ is a reporter and her boss is a lunatic, that's the job in the modern world, I don't know what she expects or wanted out of that job.

Every character is whiny and overly needy. It feels like the game wanted to take place over months or years instead of a couple days. Kraven has a militarized force trying to capture and kill people all over New York but Miles is sad Peter spent a day with Harry, Harry is sad Peter spent time with Miles, Hailey is sad Miles can't spend time with her, MJ is sad Peter didn't look at her article, Miles mom is sad he couldn't meet her new boyfriend or write his 500 word college essay, Harry doesn't care that the suit is an alien taking over Peter's mind he is just mad his daddy was nice to Peter, and Miles is whining about going a day without getting a call from Peter while Peter is trying to save Harry and The Lizard, "Peter only wants to save his friend and Connors, why won't he help me find my super villain we have no leads on?" When Peter gets the symbiote suit everyone very quickly learns what it is they still seem more effected by Peter being slightly mean to them than the actual threat it might possess. Ganke is the only constantly decent functioning person in this game.

The symbiote suit itself is treated with kids gloves in a way that never makes it seem threatening. People complain about Peter causing added destruction by going around in the suit chasing after Kraven and his targets, except this is always just clearly Jameson style propaganda now coming from his friends because Peter never does anything to make these situations worse. He more aggressively saves people during an event that already has someone smashing through New York while being chased by Kraven's drone, APC, rocket launcher, etc using army. Peter really can't make things worse and visually doesn't but people want to act like he does. Part of the issues is that, even though people can die in this game, the civilians are always said to be fined during these hugely destructive, explosive, building and vehicle destroy chase spectacle moments. Half of New York could explode and someone will drop a comment about, "Good thing no one was killed." Every major building could collapse and they would add some 4Kids DBZ line where MJ says, "I can see their parachutes, looks like everyone is ok." One problem is that the first game clearly had some far right wing lunatic writing for some content and combat barks because in the first game Peter loves the police, he loved helping the police create a spy network to watch citizens, he'd fight Riker's inmates and go on Punisher rants about eradicating the criminal scum that plagues society. All the symbiote stuff is tame when compared to that, but now he is scary because he can be a little rude. Peter still actively saves people, you land on the street you can pose for pictures and high-five his fans. You get imbecilic comments from Danika's podcast, "Spiderman is in black now more black than the other black one and I don't like it, because it seems scary" where Peter hasn't even visually done anything different other than changing outfits from their point of view. While this series has unfortunately removed the humanity of Jameson, his moments were at least entertaining unlike Danika who shouldn't even be in the game and it is unfortunate that they split their time covering events. In some of the fight scenes Peter absolutely should have killed the people he fought with when under the effects of the symbiote but even then it won't go that far and never really creates an issue. I can only imagine the news reports in the next game, "Carnage and his crew went on a rampage through the city, luckily only minor cuts and bruises were reported."

It also become a frequent occurrence that problems only come from the narrative just deciding that Peter and Miles Spider-Sense just didn't want to work and that most of the scenes in the game meant to be more emotional come from rehashing the better moments from the first game. Where the story does tend to do better are usually the few moments with Miles dealing with issues in Harlem. The ability to switch between Peter and Miles most of the time also gives a few side missions that can be done with either one of them that can lead to differences with the dialogue.

On the positive side this is the best way to experience swinging around New York as Peter and Miles. There is a lot of detail in the city, the city itself has been further expanded with new areas (Queens and Brooklyn), added detail, and the ability to show more people and vehicles. Although, Coney Island is a pretty big disappointment in both the main mission taking place there and traveling to it later, would have been nice to see some interactivity there or more effect in what was available in the mission (or just not having it at all really). The mechanics of traversing the city have also been improved on with a few new skills, most notably a new wing suit that can be quickly activated deactivated and used to quickly fly through air tunnels and to traverse over water. The subway travel has been removed, a shame that some of the funny scenes that come from that are gone, but instead you get a fast travel system where you can not only place yourself near any spot on the map but you travel almost seamlessly as soon as you activate the feature and almost immediately regain control as your character appears swinging or using the new wings in the new area.

Gameplay-wise, if you’ve played the previous two titles, you’ll feel right at home. The core mechanics are still as smooth and satisfying as ever, especially the web-swinging and combat. On top of that, the new mechanics and features they’ve added make the experience feel fresh without straying too far from what made the earlier games fun.

That said, it’s not all perfect. Be aware that performance can be hit-or-miss at times. I didn’t personally run into any stuttering, but I did experience some pretty major FPS drops, and a few cutscene freezes about halfway through. Luckily, I found a helpful fix on the Steam forums that solved the issue for me:

All in all, despite these technical hiccups, I’m really enjoying the game so far and would still recommend it — especially if you’re a Spider-Man fan. Fingers crossed the devs patch out the remaining issues soon

This game was an absolute perfection and improvement from the previous 2 games. The combat was great, especially when fighting with other Marvel characters where you could do special takedowns with them. I thought that was really cool. I finally got to live out my dream of playing as Symbiote Spider Man and this game even exceeded my expectations in that regard. Seeing Peter become increasingly taken over by the Symbiote was really cool and I really felt different when I was playing that part of the story.

Venom is also my favorite character in Marvel comics and I feel like they did him justice in this game. I would say my favorite part of the whole game and all of of the Spider Man games so far was the Venom level. I did not expect to get to fight a boss. That was a lot of fun. The only critique I had of anything Venom related was the fact that it is intrinsic to his character to be immune/invisible to Spider Man's spider-sense. I feel like this could have led to some interesting gameplay experiences that never got explored. While playing this game over the course of a few weeks, I learned that there were significant cuts from this game which is unfortunate. I heard about additional Venom levels, takedowns where you could get 2 enemies at once (just like the final talent in the stealth skill tree), and at some point Miles was supposed to get the Symbiote. That being said, I am happy with what this game included.

Overall I thought this game was great. I thought that this was a combinq5ion of some already established Spider Man stories, namely Kraven's Last Hunt, along with some original ideas that worked really well. I am excited to see how this trilogy will be concluded, namely leaving the 2 remaining big bads to be Green Goblin and Carnage. Insomniac has a unique opportunity to somehow get Norman the Carnage symbiote and perhaps make him the final boss or perhaps the final phase of the Green Goblin final boss fight in order to prevent the same sort of story beat that happened at the end of this game. I have high hopes for Spider Man 3, and I would recommend this game to anyone.

There are some technical hiccups, unfortunately. But it's still a great game with polished mechanics and a good story and tons of great action. I'm playing on an AMD RX 9070xt and AMD Ryzen 7800x3d with 32 GB of RAM and there's a wide range of FPS. Sometimes above 120 and at times have dipped below 60. Game still needs some optimization love.

Where to begin, Spider-Man 2 by Insomniac Games is a refreshing game that I wish ran better on my system. Maybe I'm just stickler but this is the first Spider-man game where my PC had to run it below maxed out settings, with all the new "tech" DLSS being set to balanced and a dire need to use FG to sustain 60+ fps.

Now I know most folks are fine with FG and DLSS, but I myself hate FG. DLSS is more bearable but, FG on paper gives me more FPS but the game does not to feel like it runs at 60+FPS. Now I understand its a big game, but it shouldn't be this difficult to run on a 3080 system with 32gb of ram. I expect on the bare minimum for my game to perform at 60+FPS without the need of FG on medium settings. Not to mention the crashes.....

With that being said this game scratches every itch that I have had since finishing Miles Morales. Now its a little hard to swallow the price for me given the poor/inadequate performance, but if you can look past that and suffer through the FPS drops this game is breathtaking, from the world to the first hour of game-play. This game trys to captivate you at every corner, with its beautiful graphics and environment. This time around NYC actually feels alive with many different interactions and quests.

EDIT: About 15 hours more into the game since I wrote my initial review. 39% completed through the story and countless trial and error phases later. I finally found a sweet spot in my graphics setting which doesn't jeopardise my visual fidelity and give me a consistent FPS between 75 and 150 FPS depending on the situation is. Now I love enjoying games at a high fidelity while having a consistent and performing FPS. With DLSS set to Quality, FG turned on, RT set to high (No shadows/AO RT) and everything else set to high I am very much enjoying the experience. Even with my RTX 3080 10gb, i9-9900k and 32gb ram... Much better performance than I intially had.

Let me start my review by saying the story (NO SPOILERS) is absolutely amazing. As someone who has played the first 2 games in this series, I was blown away how they kept having climactic moment after climactic moment. One of the best superhero experiences I've ever played.

That said, before I bought the game I read the other reviews about the poor optimization and thought, "A few screen tears and pop in animations are fine I'll deal with it." Well, that was a mistake. This game played HORRIBLE on my fairly decent computer (3070 TI graphics). The first 50% of the game wasn't too bad, but towards the end game it was extremely choppy and I even failed a boss fight because the frames were so bad and it didn't register my button presses fast enough. That was a terrible experience. On top of that the audio crackled and at times was pathetically bad. I'm talking you almost cant understand the dialog bad.

I wished I had waited to just play this on PS5 when I get one for GTA6 (lets be real, lots of us PC gamers are going to do this) If your in the same boat to Vice City as I am - honestly just wait to play it on a much better optimized system. It will be WELL worth the wait.

WHAT A RIDE! I've played pretty much all the Spider-man games since "Spider-man 2" on Playstation 1 (yes, I'm a 90's kid). I really hope DLC's will still be released for this one. If they're on this level, I'd get them in an instant.
I'm very curious for what the Wolverine project will be, because if it's made with this amount of love it will surely be incredible.

Pros: - Traversal is still a lot of fun, it's like a game within a game
- Great visuals and immersive map
- Stellar performance from the actors
- Parry is pretty cool
- Even though it's mostly just mashing, combat is still fun, I really like the new special abilities and the teamup moves, and it's so fun to learn the moves
- Stealth combat is still the best part, you can now shoot webs to make your own ledges
- It's easier to use your gadgets, last game you kinda had to frantically swap between them during combat, now they're all mapped to buttons
- Super fun characters (except MJ, see below)
- Alt skins are also super cool (except Miles' goofy one at the end), there's no more suit powers but I never really used them anyways, plus the new special abilities are way better
- Some fun side missions and interesting side stories that expand the universe with other minor Spider-Man villains
- You can still skip puzzles if you want, and most of them are in optional side quests. The mandatory ones are fine
-
- They fixed the performance issues (granted they should not have been there in the first place, but at least they fixed them)
- Absolute cinema boss fights
-

Cons: - Although it's easier to use your gadgets, they removed a bunch of them in order to have the new abilities which limits your options, especially during stealth
- 3D models can still get janky and can cause softlocks
- Still no way to manually lock onto enemies (other than attacking them)
- Camera still sucks
- NPC behaviour is still annoying, distracting, and inconsistent: casual swinging causes them to freak out and trip very easily, while a fight can break out in front of them and they don't react at all or they have a very delayed reaction
- Side content was way better in the two previous games. While they do have their own really fun interesting tie-in mini-stories, most of the side missions are either copy/pasted rinse and repeat chores, crappy errands or extremely boring puzzles, so while it's good that I can skip a puzzle I can't figure out, there is zero point in playing these side quests because I can just skip through everything.
- Flame Cultists are underwhelming as villains
- Some rushed or boring story beats and arcs
- Not sure why they changed the car chase missions, they're just QTEs now with almost zero gameplay
-
- They kept the regular human missions (especially MJ missions which are still there for some reason but aren't nearly as bad)

Overall, the main content is generally good, but certain aspects of the game (like the generally sub-par side content and some of the story) are VERY rushed, coupled with some super boring regular human missions 7.5/10

Absolutely loved this game. If you enjoyed Spider-Man (2018), there's a very high chance you'll love Spider-Man 2 just as much—if not more in some areas.

The gameplay is where Spider-Man 2 truly shines. Swinging through the city feels better than ever, and the combat is tight, responsive, and packed with cool abilities that make every encounter fun. Both Peter and Miles have distinct playstyles that keep things fresh throughout the story. Traversal has also been improved with new features that make moving around New York a complete joy.

The graphics are stunning—truly a showcase of what Insomniac can do visually. From detailed character models to the beautifully rendered city, everything looks and feels next-gen.

Story-wise, while it might not hit quite as hard as the first game emotionally, it’s still a strong narrative with some great character moments and set pieces. It kept me invested all the way through.

In terms of content, there may be slightly less than the previous entry, which is a bit noticeable. This is likely due to Insomniac choosing not to pursue DLC after the unfortunate leaks, which is understandable. That said, what is here is polished, meaningful, and well worth the time.

I played for 32 hours and reached 100% completion without running into many bugs at all. Performance was smooth the entire time, making for a really solid experience from start to finish.

Overall, Spider-Man 2 is a fantastic game that delivers where it counts. Great gameplay, amazing visuals, strong (if slightly lighter) story, and impressive polish. Highly recommended.

A horribly Ported Game. It took me 30 minutes for the game to even load up and start. Once it did, the cut-scenes froze repeatedly, and even made the characters freeze as well as T-pose within them and go out of sync with the dialogue. The audio even constantly cut out within the cut scenes. On top of that, there were many glitches that cause the game to bug out and make my character go out of bounds and break the mission which forced constant restarts. This was all in the very first mission of the game and was downright comical in how broken it was. The funny thing is, when I was in combat the game ran fine, and I had no frame drops or hiccups. However, whenever a cutscene or scripted event happens, it totally breaks. I have no idea how some people got this game to run on their Pc's. I have an RTX 4080 with 32 GB of RAM and it can't even handle it.

I think Spider-Man 2 is overall a great game and I had a ton of fun playing it, however, I do have only a few issues with it. While I did find the story fun to follow, you can tell that it had to be really condensed down due to time constraints. For example, I think Kraven is handled pretty well overall, he’s properly badass and shown to be a threatening menace as he should, but then his story kinda just stops abruptly and he’s barely even a thing and the latter of the story and you can tell they really wanted to do more with him, but just couldn’t. There are serval moments in the story like this, and this game really could’ve benefited from an extra year of development. Also, I know it’s a very common opinion, but I do believe Venom is just handled very poorly in this game, as a HUGE fan of the character I feel like the Venom in this game is Venom in name only and is nothing like the Venom I know and love. Now I don’t blame Insomniac for these faults, (except for maybe the poorly handled and written Venom), they clearly wanted to do a lot for this game but got hit hard by higher-ups deadlines and just made do with what they already had. Now with all this said I did still really love the game. The story even with all its faults is still really fun to go through, and I just love these characters. The combat and mobility that made me fall in love with the first game are still here and as great as ever. At the end of the day, I love Spider-Man and think the game was still a treat to play and still highly recommend it to any fellow Spidey heads.

all around worse game than 1, in almost every aspect. removed a lot of fun mechanics even like throwing car doors. also a lot of bugs. buggy too: clipped into walls and had to reset at least 5 times, stood on geometry which wasn't there, etc.

Story: I went into this game expecting a very mid-story especially since Miles Morales's story was pretty cringe, predictable, and just not as good as SM1, I play it and the story is about what I expected in the beginning, nothing mind-blowing, kinda dull, predictable writing, then the symbiote comes into play which feels like a completely different and better script was at hand, then after the symbiote leaves Peter the story takes another downturn because at that point it's like the writers did not want to make it a Spider-Man game but wanted Miles Morales to be the main character, The fact that Yuri just quit being a cop and became a vigilante with a hook is also pretty stupid. The app of Miles Morales also returned which replaces Spiderman tunning into police frequencies and finding people in danger which is the most ridiculous and dumbest thing in the entire game, I can't look past the fact that if such an app existed it would be used to troll and prank spiderman, and the whole fight with Mary Jane and her talking about her book that nobody bought felt like such a self-insert moment where a particular writer wanted to vent. Overall the story is better than MM but not as good as SM1. Feels like SBI touched the beginning and end of the script but kept the middle intact which is why it felt too good.

Gameplay: Its gameplay is more or less of the same except now you can parry which adds a little more to the combat rather than just dodging, the stealth takedowns are better than ever, and the swinging is great (when you turn off the hand holding settings of swing assist) you can also glide like Batman and get crazy speed, making it hard to ever play the previous games, unfortunately, the game has probably some of the worst side quests since they're so boring, and tedious, by the end I didn't feel nearly as satisfied collecting all the collectibles like when I played Spiderman 1 or MM.

Conclusion: You play these games for their gameplay first, and story second, you come in expecting a wild story you'll get something in the midpoint but then it disappoints the way it wishes Peter wasn't the main character. I give this game a solid 7/10, Spiderman: New York Knight

The game has been running well on my computer. Nonetheless, I had to greatly reduce the quality of the game in order to minimise lag. In particular, I lowered the graphics to "LOW" and the resolution to 2K. Though reduced in quality, I have not run into any serious lags or game breaking glitches since. Sometimes the framerate is reduced during cutscenes, but not too often (also, their eyes in the dark during cutscenes are strange and you cannot read anything written on paper in the game). Definitely not as bad as I was expecting, given the comments and poor reception at launch.

My computer: 32gb RAM, AMD Ryzen 7 5700X 8-Core Processor (3.40 GHz), NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060

This is truly dare I say it a perfect sequel. The characters changed compared to the first game design wise but not in an unnatural way. The story is magnificent and feels like playing a comic book. Combat is super satisfying once you got some upgrades and abilities going but can be a bit much at first. But let's speak about the elephant in the room. The performance of this game is utter garbage at times. I tried so many things to get it running but the endless stuttering of cutscenes and sometimes audio delay could not be stopped. Even when I set all my graphics on low it still lagged like crazy at times. This is a huge bummer because other than that this game would be a solid 9/10. Would definetely recommend if you're a spiderman fan and if you can stand the lag that will most likely still occur!

The story in this game is disappointing, Kraven is absolutely wasted potential and Venom is rushed through and through. Gameplay has been nerfed through removing suit abilites and gadgets (Now you can only use 4 gadgets instead of a wheel of 8). The world is super cluttered with ui elements leaking into the open world, idk why we need big vibrant circles telling us where we can use the webwings. Final complaint, why are there only 3 symbiote suits and why are you locked into using the default suit for half the game? This game is supposed to be the ultimate customizable experience, there is no customization when your suit is locked for half of the story.

This game is amazing for mods though, with the implementation of flight, you can mod in any flying strong hero and create your own open world experience using them. You can also set any suit to be your venom suit aswell as a mod that lets you switch to your venom suit during gameplay.

This game has amazing mods, and I would only recommend it both on sale and modded
